June 6 to 7, 2026 · Hiawatha National Forest

Two-day gravel omnium

Iron & Pine Omnium

Two days of gravel riding in the Upper Peninsula.

Saturday starts with MK Ultra Gravel. Sunday finishes at Hiawatha's Revenge with timed sectors and KOM points. The omnium keeps separate overall standings for women, men, and non-binary riders.

See the Omnium Leaderboard How the weekend works

Saturday ride

Start with distance, groups, and moving time.

Sunday push

Finish with timed sectors, climbs, and a harder sort.

Separate standings

Women, men, and non-binary riders each get their own overall table.

Weekend overview

Ride Saturday. Push Sunday. Add it up at the end.

Format Fondo + Grinduro
Scoring Moving time · sectors · KOM
Fields Women · Men · Non-Binary

Saturday

Day 1 · MK Ultra Gravel

Long gravel day with moving time counted toward the overall.

Open companion site

Sunday

Day 2 · Hiawatha's Revenge

Timed sectors and KOM points decide more of the table.

Open companion site

What To Expect

A simple page for a two-day ride weekend.

The companion sites carry the route details. This page keeps the overview, the combined scoring, and photos that actually show the roads.

Forest roads

The route photos do most of the talking: pines, water, hardpack, and loose rock.

Simple weekend shape

Two established events, one combined result, and an easy page to check between ride days.

Stay around

It should feel like a ride weekend people want to hang around for, not just race through.

From The Route

Photos from both days.

Pulled directly from the MK Ultra Gravel and Hiawatha's Revenge sites.

Weekend Flow

Start long, finish sharp.

The first day rewards steady riding. The second day puts more weight on timed sectors and climbing.

Saturday

Day 1 · MK Ultra Gravel

Long gravel day with moving time counted toward the overall.

MK Ultra Gravel opens the weekend with the bigger miles and sets the first benchmark without deciding everything.

Visit companion site

Sunday

Day 2 · Hiawatha's Revenge

Timed sectors and KOM points decide more of the table.

Hiawatha's Revenge brings the sharper efforts and carries the heaviest scoring weight into the finish.

Visit companion site

Scoring System

Straightforward scoring.

Each category is benchmarked against its fastest rider, then weighted across the weekend.

35% weighted contribution

Day 1 moving time

Each rider is benchmarked against the fastest moving time in their category. The opener matters, but it does not overpower the rest of the weekend.

45% weighted contribution

Day 2 gravel sectors

All timed gravel sectors are totaled, benchmarked against the fastest category aggregate, and weighted most heavily to reflect the grinduro pressure.

20% weighted contribution

Day 2 KOM points

Climb points reward riders who race aggressively, even if they are not the absolute fastest across every gravel sector.

Formula

Overall score = day 1 benchmark ratio × 35 + day 2 sector benchmark ratio × 45 + KOM benchmark ratio × 20

Each category is ranked independently. The leaderboard below uses sample rider data so the model and UI can be validated before importing real results.

Leaderboard

Sample standings.

This table still uses sample riders while the format and scoring are being dialed in.

Live Omnium Model

Overall winners are calculated inside each category.
